methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $130M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Infrastructure & Logistics (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 1 same-sector peers exist in Infrastructure & Logistics, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Amperon
private.rationale: Same sector (Infrastructure & Logistics), same stage (Series B)

data.narrative:
Earth AI, a Series B infrastructure and logistics company founded by Roman Teslyuk, has raised $20M to date. The valuation landscape for this sector remains sparse; only two private peers (AiDash and Amperon) are tracked, with no public comparables available. This scarcity of same-stage Infrastructure & Logistics peers precludes a robust peer-median approach—the dataset contains insufficient depth to support triangulation via multiples.

Stage-progression methodology is the primary applicable lens. Using total capital raised ($20M) as a base value proxy and normalizing to $130M, the analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B to next-stage transitions, yielding $247M. Sensitivity around this step-up—acknowledging variance in growth trajectories and market conditions—supports a range of $210M (low) to $284M (high), with $247M as the median estimate.

The core uncertainty is the composition and trajectory of Earth AI's revenue relative to the sparse peer set. Without sufficient same-stage comparables in the Infrastructure & Logistics sector, this valuation relies entirely on stage-based progression mechanics rather than market-derived benchmarks. Further clarity on unit economics, customer concentration, and gross margins would refine conviction in the range. The $210M–$284M band should be treated as directional rather than definitive absent richer peer visibility.
